contexts: The abstract discusses the EGFR signaling pathway, its interaction with the ligand EGF, and the ongoing development of inhibitors for cancer treatment.
The abstract reviews the canonical and emerging noncanonical functions of the EGFR in physiological processes and its role as a driver of tumorigenesis in various cancers.
The abstract discusses the history and methodologies of covalent drug discovery, specifically mentioning the development of covalent inhibitors for targets such as EGFR and BTK.

The abstract reviews the mechanisms of resistance to anti-EGFR monoclonal antibodies, such as cetuximab and panitumumab, in metastatic colorectal cancer and explores strategies to overcome this resistance.
The study characterizes how different endogenous ligands like epiregulin and epigen induce varied EGFR dimerization strengths and signaling dynamics to drive distinct cellular responses.
